title: What do we know for sure about Rebuild canon
display_title: What do we know for sure about Rebuild canon
wikitext:
[]: What do we know for sure about Rebuild canon?             Quote Post by Shay Guy » Wed Dec 10, 2008 7:10 pm And what parts of our knowledge of the TV series -- humongous continuity snarl that it is -- might not apply? Is the Classified Information even applicable? In other words, if Rebuild was all there was of the franchise -- meaning right now, 1.0 would be all there was -- what would we know, and what would be suspected? (I'm willing to allow some cheats, like Kaworu's name, which was never used in his scene.)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 10, 2008 7:58 pm Evangelion =/= Rebuild. Anything goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Dec 10, 2008 8:15 pm We know about as much as you at this point but we all have are theory's and hopes.

[]:             Quote Post by Shay Guy » Wed Dec 10, 2008 9:47 pm Yeah, I know. Everyone who's seen 1.0 has pretty much all the relevant info. I just thought it might be useful to have a place to go over what's for certain and what's up in the air. Problem is, listing the known facts would be REALLY tedious. Let's see... There is a UN-supervised organization called NERV which is fighting beings called "Angels." Three of these have been seen attacking Tokyo-3, the city where NERV's headquarters is located. They have been labeled the fourth, fifth, and sixth Angels; the first and third are unaccounted for, but a large being hidden in NERV's headquarters has been identified as the second, "Lilith." They seem to be impervious to most conventional weaponry, thanks apparently to "AT Fields" that protect them; even the formidable "N2 Mine" posed only a minor inconvenience when it was used. Therefore, NERV fights them with giant biomechanical humanoids called "Evangelions," piloted by selected humans through a neural interface (though they are capable of "going berserk" and acting on their own); the Evas are also capable of producing AT Fields and using them to neutralize the effects of the Angels'. Two of the Eva pilots, Shinji Ikari (the "Third Child") and Rei Ayanami, have been seen so far, though more are promised to appear (including Unit 02's pilot in Germany). NERV is run by Gendo Ikari, Shinji's father, though he reports to a mysterious organization called SEELE which has made reference to killing the Angels as part of a "contract with Lilith." ...Okay, that's all I have the patience for right now. Most of this, of course, is pretty much the same as in the series; I haven't even gotten to Kaworu. Like I said, I'm not sure this is even worth the effort, but it may help stimulate some lines of thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Dec 10, 2008 10:00 pm You can fill in the content here: http://evageeks.org/FGP/Differences_between_Evangelion_1.0_and_Neon_Genesis_Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 11, 2008 1:21 am Sachi_13 wrote: Evangelion =/= Rebuild. Anything goes.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Dec 11, 2008 1:37 am Reichu wrote: Sachi_13 wrote: Evangelion =/= Rebuild. Anything goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Dec 11, 2008 1:38 am In Rebuild canon, Misato and Shinji will boink in the most graphical manner that current animation technology available will allow. Also, Kaworu will be confirmed to indeed have an interest in Shinji's penis. Seriously though, with the early introduction and identification of Lilith in the first movie, it's safe to say that the Rebuild canon and the original canon only share basic details for sure, ex. Shinji pilots the purple eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 11, 2008 2:05 am Reichu wrote: "Rebuild" isn't "Evangelion"? Then what is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 11, 2008 2:12 am Sigged.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 11, 2008 4:30 am Reichu wrote: Thundercats?

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Dec 11, 2008 5:01 pm well, if this is an new canon we cant 100% for sure say Misato was telling the truth about lilith being in Level EEE lilith could be the one on the moon and adam in Terminal dogma? I think thats correct logically

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Thu Dec 11, 2008 5:16 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Reichu wrote: "Rebuild" isn't "Evangelion"? Then what is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 11, 2008 5:43 pm Chrad wrote: Did you take that from 4chan or was that you?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 11, 2008 6:34 pm Maybe it is new canon but even if it is, who cares cause it will still be amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 11, 2008 6:49 pm If that Tomino mecha series that was later redone as a film was a brand new canon (I forget the name; hons mentioned on IRC & apparently it had a happy ending compared to its series), then Rebuild probably is, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Dec 11, 2008 6:57 pm If it's Ideon, the movies were the same as Death and EoE in comparison. Not Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 11, 2008 7:09 pm It wasn't Ideon but possibly a Tomino work.

[]:             Quote Post by Furu » Fri Dec 12, 2008 4:51 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: It wasn't Ideon but possibly a Tomino work.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Fri Dec 12, 2008 5:56 pm Both Macross and Escaflowne were remade with new canons. What exactly could Rebuild be if not a separate canon? Besides a sequel of course.
